name: Upstash Redis MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Upstash MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Upstash's serverless Redis and Kafka — Redis data operations, database management, and Kafka topic interactions with per-request pricing.
use_cases: Storing and retrieving agent context/memory in serverless Redis
Rate limiting implementation via Upstash Redis in agent workflows
Managing Upstash databases and configurations from agents
Queue and pub/sub operations via Upstash Kafka from agents
Edge-cached data access for globally distributed agent workflows
not_for: High-throughput persistent Redis workloads (Upstash has per-command pricing)
Teams requiring on-premises or VPC-only Redis
Workloads needing Redis Cluster or advanced data structure commands
best_when: An agent needs serverless, HTTP-based Redis access with pay-per-use pricing — ideal for edge deployments, low-traffic agents, or ephemeral storage.
avoid_when: You need persistent connections, Redis Cluster, or high-throughput low-latency caching at scale — use Redis Cloud directly.

auth.notes: REST API uses per-database REST tokens for data operations. Management API uses account-level API key. REST tokens provide database-level isolation.

pricing.notes: Per-command pricing makes costs predictable for low-traffic agents. Free tier sufficient for development and low-volume production.

performance.rate_limits: Per-command limits by tier; burst allowed; well-documented

known_agent_gotchas: Per-command pricing means chatty agents accumulate costs quickly — batch operations where possible
REST API adds latency vs native Redis protocol — not suitable for sub-millisecond requirements
Daily command limits on free tier hit fast in agent testing loops
Management API vs data REST API use different auth tokens — configure both
Upstash Kafka is a separate product from Redis — different configuration required
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for REST API. Per-database REST tokens provide better isolation than single API keys. TLS enforced. SOC 2 Type II.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Per-database REST tokens better than single API keys. SOC 2 Type II. Good serverless security posture.
